# Questions in Livestream 152

Live Stream #152: Good Friday 2024

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=556" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:16</a> | If God is unchanging, how could he become incarnate? | He answers with the classic two-natures framework: Christ adds a human nature without changing the divine one. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:29</a> | Could Genesis describe the start of the Anthropocene? | He says yes; he does not see a theological or historical problem with using that label in this way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=990" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:30</a> | Why were earlier rulers of Egypt, such as Mamluks and Ottomans, unable to decipher hieroglyphs? | He says knowledge of hieroglyphs and demotic had died out long before, and they lacked the linguistic tools and living tradition to recover it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=1369" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:49</a> | If Osiris may be tied to a deified person, why not say the same about Jesus? | He says the parallel fails because Osiris is divine within the mythic framework itself, not simply a human later promoted to deity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=1788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:48</a> | How was the bitter-water ordeal for adultery supposed to work? | He describes the holy water as running water handled in a ritually clean vessel, with altar material added as part of the rite. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=2208" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:48</a> | How would you justify God commanding slaughter in Deuteronomy 20:16-17 or other divine judgments on nations? | He says the passage is restricted to the cities in view and is tied to preventing idolatry, not a blanket justification for killing every Canaanite everywhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=3503" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:23</a> | What is a `toledot`, and how is it different from a genealogy? | He says a `toledot` is result-oriented: it shows what came from a person or event, not just a list of ancestors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=4294" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:34</a> | Can someone put an honorary degree on a resume? | He says it is generally poor form because an honorary degree is not an earned degree. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=4410" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:30</a> | Does Romans 13:2 mean revolutions are bad, and can `telos` mean payment in Romans 13:7? | He says Romans 13 gives a general rule discouraging rebellion, with civil disobedience only when obeying government would mean disobeying God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=5399" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:59</a> | What should someone do if they think they may have a gift of teaching but do not want to seem prideful? | He says the hesitation is common, but spiritual gifts are for building up the church and should not be buried out of fear. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=5810" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:36:50</a> | How should I correct a brother in Christ who evangelizes at work but says wrong things in front of a new coworker? | He advises caution: get to know him, ask questions privately, and remember that workplace relationships still have to remain professional. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=6159" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:39</a> | How did you and your wife meet, and how did you know she was the one? | He says they met at a science-fiction convention and that prayer, shared faith, and shared interests helped confirm the relationship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=6388" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:28</a> | Is serial monogamy sinful? | He says it depends: remarriage after a spouse's death can be legitimate, but serial monogamy outside marriage is sinful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=6553" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:13</a> | Does Judges 13:18 prove that Isaiah 9:6 should read "wonderful counselor" as one title? | He says the comparison can help, but the issue has to be handled through the Hebrew wording and context rather than a simple prooftext. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=6957" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:57</a> | How should Luke 10:38-42, with Mary and Martha, be understood? | He says Jesus corrects Martha's anxiety and honors Mary's attention to his teaching as the better portion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=7400" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:20</a> | In Ezekiel 18:4, does "the soul who sins will die" mean spiritual death or physical death? | He frames the passage first as a covenantal correction to inherited-blame thinking under the old covenant, stressing individual responsibility. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=7779" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:09:39</a> | When did the Philistines first arrive in Canaan, before or after the Exodus? | He places the main Philistine arrival after the Exodus in the late Bronze to early Iron transition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=7885" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:25</a> | How has cherubim and seraphim imagery changed over time? | He says later art and tradition often flatten or alter the biblical imagery, which is stranger and more throne-room oriented than common depictions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=8013" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:33</a> | Does Genesis 4:13-15 suggest there were other people besides Adam, Eve, Cain, and Abel? | He discusses Cain's fear and the larger family context, treating it as a question about other descendants rather than unrelated humans. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=8622" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:42</a> | Could you explain Zechariah 3? | He reads the scene around Joshua the high priest as a symbolic cleansing and restoration passage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=8963" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:23</a> | Does God creating the tree of the knowledge of good and evil make him the author of sin? | He says no; creating the possibility of obedience or disobedience is not the same as causing sin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=9890" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:50</a> | Why is the firstborn not always the biological firstborn in Scripture? | He says "firstborn" can function as a status or heirship category, not only a birth-order label. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=10233" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:33</a> | What would you say to skeptics who think divine judgment is unjust? | He says human demands for justice often become uncomfortable when applied consistently, and biblical judgment must be read within God's justice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=10522" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:22</a> | Why are Psalm 14 and Psalm 53 so similar? | He treats them as related textual traditions or reused psalm material rather than a contradiction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=10634" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:14</a> | Does Dan McClellan's claim about prose mean the Pentateuch could not have come from Moses? | He rejects the argument, saying prose writing is far older and not a reason to deny Mosaic material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=11045" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:05</a> | What does Song of Solomon 7:10-13 mean? | He reads it as poetic love language within the Song's marital and romantic imagery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=11818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:58</a> | How is Adam a type of Christ in Romans 5? | He says Adam and Christ function as covenantal representatives, with Adam bringing death and Christ bringing grace and life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=12227" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:47</a> | Was the Abydos Dynasty an Amorite dynasty? | He is skeptical and says he has not seen serious Egyptological support for that proposal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=12747" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:27</a> | If Christianity becomes mainstream in China, will China produce its own interpretation and texts as with Mahayana Buddhism? | He says state-shaped churches and translations already exist, centered on the supremacy of the Communist Party, while house churches resist that control. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=13092" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:12</a> | What about the alleged misconduct report against you? | He says he knew nothing about it and treated it as a case of mistaken identity with another David Falk. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=13397" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:17</a> | Was Jonah dead inside the fish? | He says Jonah 2 is full of grave and death language, so he leans toward reading the episode as involving real death imagery rather than a merely uncomfortable survival scene. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=13822" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:22</a> | What are your thoughts on the doctrine of ancestral sin? | He says he is not familiar with that exact label and interprets the question as likely about generational or communal guilt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=14271" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:51</a> | In Romans 13, could Paul be describing the kind of leader the passage should apply to rather than Nero? | He says Romans was written during Nero's reign, so the passage cannot be limited only to good rulers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=14777" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:06:17</a> | Were Egyptians ever skeptical and did they need to prove the existence of gods like Hapi? | He says Egyptian religion generally treated gods as visible in natural realities, such as Hapi as the Nile, so they did not frame the issue as proof in that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=16985" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:43:05</a> | Did Egyptians form relationships with crocodiles like some modern villages do? | He says not really; they had religious associations through Sobek, but also hunted crocodiles and treated them as dangerous animals. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=17328" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:48:48</a> | How did Egyptians kill crocodiles? | He says they used spears, and likely many of them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6G_FngsHgV8?t=17375" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:49:35</a> | Did Egyptians use bows? | Yes. He says Egyptians used both simple and composite bows, with arrowheads attested from very early periods. |
